launch_context: A new AI meeting-notes product launched this week for product and marketing teams.

config.launch_context: A new AI meeting-notes product launched this week for product and marketing teams.

queries.label: Reddit launch reactions
queries.query: Acme AI Notes launch Reddit user reactions objections pricing

queries.label: Social launch chatter
queries.query: Acme AI Notes launch reactions on X LinkedIn product hunt

queries.label: Reviews and comparison pages
queries.query: Acme AI Notes review alternatives complaints best AI meeting notes

queries.label: News and blog coverage
queries.query: Acme AI Notes launch news blog coverage funding users

executive_summary.summary: Found 12 normalized launch-sentiment signals. Positive: 4, negative: 8, neutral: 0. Main positive themes: useful, clean, fast, promising, helpful. Main risks: expensive, pricing, blocker, compliance, privacy.

negative.title: Builders praise the launch but ask about pricing — Reddit launch reactions
negative.snippet: Users say the workflow looks useful and fast, but several comments ask whether the paid plan is too expensive for small teams.
negative.content: Positive launch reaction. Useful automation, clean setup, fast results. Pricing concern appears repeatedly.

negative.title: Privacy questions show up in reddit/social discussion
negative.snippet: Teams like the product idea, but privacy and data retention questions are a blocker for some buyers.
negative.content: The product is promising, but privacy, compliance, and admin controls need clearer messaging.

query_summaries.label: Reddit launch reactions
query_summaries.query: Acme AI Notes launch Reddit user reactions objections pricing
query_summaries.answer: Early reddit launch reactions shows mostly positive interest with a few repeated concerns around pricing, privacy, and onboarding clarity.

recommended_follow_up_searches: Acme AI Notes pricing objections Reddit
Acme AI Notes privacy security concerns
Acme AI Notes launch user feedback product hunt
product_marketing_actions: Turn repeated objections into FAQ or launch-page copy.
Use representative positive examples as proof points if the source permits it.
Run the same config weekly to separate launch spike from persistent sentiment.
Create follow-up searches for any risk term that appears across multiple source types.
